reasoning: The FRED BofA BBB OAS index experienced a sharp spike in late February 2026, jumping from 0.96 mid-month to 1.08 by February 28. Market reporting confirms this reflects the largest spread widening in months, driven by an expected $400B debt issuance wave and market strain as IG credit hit 30-year lows (tights). Because spreads are bouncing off historical lows with a documented catalyst (oversupply), short-term momentum likely favors continued widening, pushing the Mar 8 probability above 50%. Over the medium-term (Mar 31), market stabilization and mean-reversion are more likely to offset the initial shock, increasing uncertainty and pulling the probability closer to the 50% base rate.
